headline: Russian intelligence escalated its assault on encrypted messaging by coaxing targets into surrendering Signal backup keys, while attackers weaponized a Cisco Unified CM flaw within a day of disclosure.

emerging_trends.text: Attackers keep slipping in through software repositories and third-party vendors—the Miasma worm spread across npm and into the Go ecosystem—pushing the Linux Foundation to launch its Akrites remediation framework.
emerging_trends.topic_title: Open-Source Supply Chain: Miasma Spreads as Akrites Launches

emerging_trends.text: Enterprises are scrambling to govern AI agents and machine identities, with Cisco buying Astrix and WideField and Proof launching its x401 verification protocol, even as scammers impersonate companies through fraudulent OpenAI organization invites.
emerging_trends.topic_title: AI Agents Become an Identity Battleground

emerging_trends.text: Russian intelligence services keep refining their attacks on encrypted communications, now persuading targets to surrender their Signal Backup Recovery Keys to seize entire message histories.
emerging_trends.topic_title: Russian Spies Hunt Signal Backup Keys

topics.title: Russian Spies Hunt Signal Backup Keys
topics.latest_developments: The FBI and CISA updated their March 2026 warning on June 26, revealing that Russian Intelligence Services operators now talk targets into surrendering their Signal Backup Recovery Key, which lets the attacker restore the account's backup, read its private and group history, and keep persistent access.
topics.summary: Russian intelligence runs phishing campaigns against Signal and other commercial messaging apps; Ukraine's SBU traced a parallel long-running operation in which fake tech-support workers extracted messaging credentials. Treat unsolicited device-linking or backup-key requests as hostile.

sources.title: FBI Warns Russian Intelligence Hackers Target Signal Backup Recovery Keys
Russian Intelligence Services Continue to Target Commercial Messaging Applications
Russia used social engineering to breach prominent messaging accounts, Ukraine says

topics.title: Open-Source Supply Chain: Miasma Spreads as Akrites Launches
topics.latest_developments: The Miasma worm, part of the Mini Shai-Hulud and Hades family, infected fresh npm releases of LeoPlatform and RStreams, abused GitHub Actions workflows, and crossed into the Go ecosystem, while the Linux Foundation launched Akrites, an industry framework uniting tech firms, banks, and AI companies to report, patch, and disclose open-source vulnerabilities as AI compresses the time from discovery to exploitation.
topics.summary: Self-propagating malware keeps poisoning the package registries that feed enterprise builds, and a new coordinated-disclosure framework aims to speed remediation. Pin dependencies and audit CI/CD tokens.

sources.title: Miasma Malware Targets npm Packages and GitHub Actions in Supply Chain Attack
Critical open-source projects get a new security framework
Linux Foundation Unveils New Open Source Security Project Akrites

topics.title: AI Agents Become an Identity Battleground
topics.latest_developments: Threat actors began creating OpenAI tenants that impersonate real companies and inviting employees to join, harvesting sensitive data through chats and projects, while Cisco acquired Astrix and WideField to secure non-human identities and Proof launched x401, an open protocol that verifies the identity behind AI agents before a service acts.
topics.summary: AI agents and machine identities now operate with human-level access and little oversight, drawing both attackers and a wave of governance tooling. Inventory non-human identities and scrutinize unexpected platform invitations.

sources.title: Cybersecurity firms targeted by fraudulent OpenAI organization invites
Cisco Adds NHI to Security Stack With Astrix, WideField Acquisitions
Proof's x401 establishes an open protocol for AI agent identity and authorization

topics.title: Cisco Unified CM Flaw Draws CISA Deadline
topics.latest_developments: CISA ordered federal civilian agencies to patch Cisco Unified Communications Manager flaw CVE-2026-20230 by Sunday, June 28, after attackers weaponized the server-side request forgery bug within 24 hours of disclosure to forge root-level access.
topics.summary: CVE-2026-20230 lets unauthenticated attackers reach internal services and escalate to root on Cisco Unified CM and Unified CM Session Management Edition deployments. Patch internet-facing systems immediately.

sources.title: CISA sets urgent deadline to fix Cisco flaw exploited in attacks
In Less Than 24 Hours, Attackers Weaponize Cisco CUCM Flaw

topics.title: DirtyClone Joins Linux Kernel Root Exploits
topics.latest_developments: JFrog Security Research published the first working exploit for DirtyClone, CVE-2026-43503, on June 25—a DirtyFrag-family kernel bug rated CVSS 8.8 that corrupts file-backed memory through a cloned network packet—landing days after a public exploit for pedit COW, CVE-2026-46331, gave local users root the same way.
topics.summary: Both flaws let an unprivileged local user gain root on Linux by poisoning shared page-cache memory through crafted packets. Apply kernel updates; Red Hat rates pedit COW high severity.

sources.title: New DirtyClone Linux Kernel Flaw Lets Local Users Gain Root via Cloned Packets
New Linux pedit COW Exploit Enables Root Access by Poisoning Cached Binaries

topics.title: Surveillance Tools Reach Police, Military, and Officials' Data
topics.latest_developments: Meta began prototyping real-time facial recognition with a Pentagon supplier for police and military use, and the Pentagon opened an inquiry into the Dialog data exposure after leaked records unmasked a senior White House intelligence official and an active-duty special operations officer.
topics.summary: Government-linked surveillance prototypes and data-exposure incidents are putting the personal details of national security personnel at risk. Officials and agencies face mounting privacy and operational-security exposure.

sources.title: Meta Is Testing Facial Recognition for Police and Military
The Pentagon Is Looking Into the Dialog Data Exposure for Unmasking National Security Officials

weather: Tonight: Mostly Cloudy then Slight Chance Showers And Thunderstorms, low 64F.

games.recap: Brandon Lowe, Henry Davis each homer to lead Pirates over Mariners 5-1

business_politics.title: Trump Threatens 100% Tariff Over Digital Services Taxes
business_politics.latest_developments: Trump issued a 100% tariff threat against Europe on June 26, a day after the European Union approved tariff cuts on American goods.
business_politics.summary: President Trump threatened a 100% tariff on any country that taxes United States digital services, saying the levy would override existing trade deals and putting major European economies on notice as they weigh new taxes on American tech companies.

sources.title: Trump Threatens 100% Tariff on European Countries That Impose Digital Services Tax
Trump warns of 100% tariff on countries implementing digital services tax

business_politics.title: Iran Cargo-Ship Strike Tests Ceasefire
business_politics.latest_developments: Trump on June 26 blamed Iran for a drone strike on a cargo ship in the Strait of Hormuz, calling it a foolish violation of the ceasefire.
business_politics.summary: A drone strike hit a cargo ship in the Strait of Hormuz on Thursday, snarling traffic through the world's busiest oil chokepoint and threatening President Trump's preliminary deal to wind down the Iran war, even as oil futures settled near pre-war levels.

sources.title: Trump Says Iranian Attack on Cargo Ship Was Violation of Ceasefire Deal
Oil Returns to Pre-War Level on Middle East Optimism

business.title: Cranberry Costco Starts Selling Beer and Wine
business.latest_developments: WTAE reported June 26 that the Cranberry Township Costco became the first Costco in Pennsylvania to sell beer and wine.
business.summary: The Costco warehouse in Cranberry Township, Butler County, began selling beer and wine, the first store in the chain's Pennsylvania locations to do so.

sources.title: Cranberry store is first Costco in Pennsylvania to sell beer and wine

business.title: Downtown Tax-Diversion District Draws Backlash
business.latest_developments: Residents lashed out at the proposed Golden Triangle tax-diversion district at a Thursday meeting, TribLive reported June 26.
business.summary: Pittsburgh's plan to earmark future tax revenue from new Downtown construction for Golden Triangle redevelopment met stiff opposition from residents who questioned its transparency and fairness.

sources.title: Pittsburghers lash out at proposed Downtown tax diversion district

around_town.title: Parkway East Closure Looms; PRT Urges Transit
around_town.latest_developments: Pittsburgh Regional Transit on June 26 pressed drivers to ride the East Busway during next month's three-week Parkway East closure.
around_town.summary: Pittsburgh Regional Transit is steering commuters toward public transit, especially the Wilkinsburg East Busway, ahead of a three-week Parkway East shutdown next month to replace the Commercial Street Bridge.

sources.title: PRT encourages Pittsburghers to utilize public transit during Parkway East closure

around_town.title: Overnight I-279 Closures for Bridge Demolition
around_town.latest_developments: Allegheny County released the schedule June 26 for overnight northbound I-279 closures next week.
around_town.summary: The Allegheny County Department of Public Works will close northbound I-279 overnight next week to demolish Jacks Run Bridge No. 3, which carries Jacks Run Road over the highway.

sources.title: Here's when northbound I-279 will close overnight next week for bridge demolition work

around_town.title: Pennsylvania Measles Outbreak Worsens
around_town.latest_developments: State health officials issued a strong call to action June 26 as Pennsylvania's measles count climbed.
around_town.summary: Pennsylvania health officials, declaring they will not sit back as the virus spreads, escalated their response to a worsening statewide measles outbreak.

sources.title: As Pa. measles outbreak worsens, state health officials issue strong call to action

events.title: Anthrocon Marks 20th Anniversary
events.latest_developments: Organizers confirmed June 26 that Anthrocon returns July 2-5 for its 20th anniversary.
events.summary: Anthrocon, one of the world's largest furry conventions, runs July 2 through July 5 at the David L. Lawrence Convention Center Downtown under the theme 'Critters, Cryptids and Curses,' with more than 16,000 pre-registered and an expected economic impact above $18 million.

sources.title: Anthrocon returning to Pittsburgh for 20th anniversary celebration

events.title: Danny Elfman Joins the Pittsburgh Symphony
events.latest_developments: Pittsburgh Magazine reported June 26 that Danny Elfman will perform with the Pittsburgh Symphony Orchestra at Heinz Hall.
events.summary: Composer Danny Elfman brings his 'Symphony of Chaos' to Heinz Hall, Downtown, with the Pittsburgh Symphony Orchestra, his first Pittsburgh appearance in nearly 40 years.

sources.title: Danny Elfman Brings His 'Symphony of Chaos' to Pittsburgh

events.title: Picklesburgh Adds Pickleball Courts
events.latest_developments: Organizers said June 26 that Picklesburgh will debut pickleball courts at Arts Landing this year.
events.summary: Picklesburgh, the annual Downtown food festival, runs July 16-19 and will add pickleball courts at Arts Landing with tournament play, free instructional clinics, and courts featuring commissioned public artwork.

sources.title: Picklesburgh to introduce pickleball courts at Arts Landing in Downtown Pittsburgh

around_teams.title: Pirates Activate Konnor Griffin
around_teams.latest_developments: The Pirates activated rookie shortstop Konnor Griffin from the injured list June 26 and optioned Jack Brannigan to Double-A Altoona.
around_teams.summary: Pittsburgh reinstated top prospect Konnor Griffin, sidelined since May 31 with a right forearm strain, giving its injury-thinned lineup a boost after his strong rehab stint at Altoona.

sources.title: Pirates activate Konnor Griffin from the IL, providing much-needed reinforcements

around_teams.title: Eric Ebron on Cam Heyward's Podcast
around_teams.latest_developments: Former tight end Eric Ebron joined 'Not Just Football with Cam Heyward' in an episode posted June 24.
around_teams.summary: On 'Not Just Football with Cam Heyward,' Eric Ebron walked through his NFL journey across Detroit, Indianapolis, and Pittsburgh, his retirement at 28, and Andrew Luck's career.

sources.source: Not Just Football with Cam Heyward
sources.title: Eric Ebron Talks Andrew Luck's Retirement, The 11-0 Steelers Team, & More! | Not Just Football

around_teams.title: Pirates Celebrate Don Kelly's 100th Win
around_teams.latest_developments: The Post-Gazette reported June 26 that the Pirates marked manager Don Kelly's 100th career win.
around_teams.summary: Pittsburgh players and staff celebrated manager Don Kelly reaching 100 career wins, with teammates voicing hope that more follow in a postseason push.

sources.title: 'He deserves it': The Pirates celebrate Don Kelly's 100th win

team_usa.title: USMNT Falls to Türkiye in Group Finale
team_usa.latest_developments: The United States lost 3-2 to Türkiye on June 25, with Pochettino defiantly noting the team still won Group D.
team_usa.summary: A second-string United States side, already through as Group D winners, fell 3-2 to Türkiye, and coach Mauricio Pochettino sparred with reporters afterward as Christian Pulisic returned from a calf injury before the round-of-32 match against Bosnia-Herzegovina.

sources.title: Poch puzzled by 'weird' questions: We 'won group'

team_usa.title: Pochettino, US Soccer in Extension Talks
team_usa.latest_developments: Sources told ESPN on June 26 that Pochettino and U.S. Soccer are holding positive talks over a contract extension.
team_usa.summary: United States men's coach Mauricio Pochettino and U.S. Soccer have opened positive discussions about extending his contract as the co-hosts head into the World Cup knockout rounds.

sources.title: Sources: Poch in positive talks over USMNT deal

team_usa.title: Milan Rejects NYCFC Bid for Pulisic
team_usa.latest_developments: AC Milan named Massimo Calvelli chief executive June 26, a day after rejecting New York City FC's approach for Christian Pulisic.
team_usa.summary: AC Milan turned down New York City FC's approach for United States attacker Christian Pulisic and installed Massimo Calvelli as chief executive in a bid to restore a winning culture.

sources.title: Milan name CEO amid Pulisic transfer approach

reading.author: Ed Zitron
reading.title: Cargo Culture
reading.summary: Zitron argues that much of the tech industry imitates the surface rituals of successful companies without grasping the substance, a cargo-cult instinct he ties to the AI boom and its biggest names.

reading.author: Stratechery
reading.title: An Interview with Figma CEO Dylan Field About Design and AI
reading.summary: Ben Thompson interviews Figma chief executive Dylan Field on building the company and why Field believes AI gives Figma a tailwind rather than a threat.

reading.author: Cal Newport
reading.title: Dear AI Companies: Stop the "Doom Trolling"
reading.summary: Newport argues that AI firms undercut their own credibility by issuing alarming warnings about their own products, likening it to a carmaker fear-mongering about the dangers of its best-selling truck.

sports_media: Not Just Football with Cam Heyward
